segments.text: First listen, and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What was the main objective of early mountain climbers?

translations.zh-Hans: 早期登山者的主要目标是什么？

segments.text: Modern alpinists try to climb mountains by a route which will give them good sport, and the more difficult it is, the more highly it is regarded.

translations.zh-Hans: 现代登山者设法选择富有挑战乐趣的路线攀登，路线越困难，评价就越高。

segments.text: In the pioneering days, however, this was not the case at all.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而在开创时期，情况完全不是这样。

segments.text: The early climbers were looking for the easiest way to the top, because the summit was the prize they sought, especially if it had never been attained before.

translations.zh-Hans: 早期登山者寻找的是最容易登顶的路线，因为他们追求的奖赏就是峰顶，特别是从未有人登上过的峰顶。

segments.text: It is true that during their explorations they often faced difficulties and dangers of the most perilous nature.

translations.zh-Hans: 诚然，在探险过程中，他们经常面对极其危险的困难与险境。

segments.text: Equipped in a manner which would make a modern climber shudder at the thought.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们的装备方式，会让现代登山者一想到就不寒而栗。

segments.text: But they did not go out of their way to court such excitement.

translations.zh-Hans: 但他们并不会刻意寻求这种刺激。

segments.text: They had a single aim, a solitary goal, the top.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们只有一个目的、唯一的目标：峰顶。

segments.text: It is hard for us to realize nowadays how difficult it was for the pioneers.

translations.zh-Hans: 如今我们很难想象先驱者当时有多么艰难。

segments.text: Except for one or two places, such as Zermatt and Chamonix, which had rapidly become popular, alpine villages tended to be impoverished settlements cut off from civilization

translations.zh-Hans: 除采尔马特和夏蒙尼等一两个迅速走红的地方外，阿尔卑斯山村往往是贫困的聚落，被高山与文明世界

segments.text: Such inns, as there were, were generally dirty and flea-ridden.

translations.zh-Hans: 即使有客栈，通常也是肮脏不堪、跳蚤横行。

segments.text: The food simply local cheese accompanied by bread, often twelve months old, all washed down with coarse wine.

translations.zh-Hans: 食物只有当地奶酪配面包，面包常已存放十二个月，再用粗劣的葡萄酒送下肚。

segments.text: Often a valley boasted no inn at all, and climbers found shelter wherever they could, sometimes with the local priest, who was usually as poor as his parishioners, sometimes with shepherds or cheese-makers.

translations.zh-Hans: 山谷里常常连一家客栈也没有，登山者只能随处寻找栖身之所：有时投宿于当地牧师家中，而牧师通常和教区居民一样贫穷；有时住在牧羊人或奶酪制造者那里。

segments.text: Invariably the background was the same, dirt and poverty, and very uncomfortable.

translations.zh-Hans: 环境总是一样：肮脏、贫困，而且极不舒适。

segments.text: For men accustomed to eating seven-course dinners and sleeping between fine linen sheets at home, the change to the Alps must have been very hard indeed.

translations.zh-Hans: 对于在家习惯吃七道菜晚宴、睡在精细亚麻床单中的人来说，来到阿尔卑斯山后的变化一定极其难熬。
